findings.title: Multiple instances of outline:none without focus replacement
findings.description: Multiple CSS rules remove the default focus outline with `outline: none` without providing a replacement visible focus indicator. This makes it impossible for keyboard-only users and those using focus-visible browser extensions to navigate the interface effectively.
findings.evidence: outline: none; (appears 10+ times in CSS and inline styles throughout admin.html)
findings.impact: Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, making keyboard navigation extremely difficult or impossible. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (AA).

findings.title: Missing <label> elements for form inputs (admin.html)
findings.description: Many form inputs in the admin panel use only placeholder text for labels, with no associated <label> element. This makes the form inaccessible to screen reader users and creates confusion about input purpose.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add explicit <label> elements before or associated with each input:
  <label for="wiz-gallery-name">Gallery name</label>
  <input type="text" id="wiz-gallery-name" placeholder="e.g. Smith Wedding" autocomplete="off">
findings.evidence:
<input type="text" id="wiz-gallery-name" placeholder="e.g. Smith Wedding" autocomplete="off">
(No associated <label> element)
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot reliably determine the purpose of form inputs. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard (A) and 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions (A).

findings.title: Image with empty alt text on lightbox
findings.description: The lightbox image element has an empty alt attribute. While the image is displayed on demand, the empty alt text should be intentional for truly decorative images, not for functional UI images.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Either:
1. If the image should have no description (truly decorative): add aria-hidden="true" to suppress screen reader announcement
2. If the image should be described: dynamically set the alt attribute via JavaScript when the image source is set:
   document.getElementById('lightbox-img').alt = photoDescription;
findings.impact: The alt text is empty, which may be intentional for a dynamically-loaded image, but without context, screen reader users will get no description when the image loads. This violates WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content (A).

findings.title: Gallery card button lacks complete keyboard interaction
findings.description: The gallery card uses role="button" with onclick and onkeydown handlers, but the keyboard implementation only handles Enter and Space keys. Modern keyboard event handling should also consider Escape and other context-specific keys.
findings.evidence: <div class="gallery-card" role="button" tabindex="0" onclick="window.location.href=${...}" onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter'||event.key===' '){event.preventDefault();window.location.href=${...}}" aria-label="View gallery: ${...}">
findings.impact: While basic keyboard navigation works, users may expect standard button behavior (e.g., Escape to cancel in certain contexts). Inconsistent keyboard behavior may confuse keyboard-only users. Partially violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard (A).

findings.title: Admin.html missing skip navigation link
findings.description: The admin panel (admin.html) lacks a skip-to-content link. Gallery.html and other pages have a skip link, but the admin panel does not. This makes it harder for keyboard users to skip repetitive navigation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a skip link at the beginning of the body, before the nav: Skip to main content And add id="main-content" to the main content area:
findings.evidence: No <a href="#main" class="skip-link"> element found at the start of the body.
findings.impact: Keyboard users must tab through all navigation tabs before reaching the main content. This violates WCAG 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks (A).

findings.title: Potential color contrast issue with --text-dim color
findings.description: The --text-dim CSS variable (#8a7f74 on dark background #050505) may not meet the 4.5:1 contrast ratio requirement for normal text. This affects readability for low-vision users.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Increase the lightness of --text-dim to improve contrast:
  Current: --text-dim: #8a7f74;
  Suggested: --text-dim: #a89a8c; (or similar lighter shade)

Verify the new ratio meets 4.5:1 using a contrast checker.
findings.evidence:
--text-dim: #8a7f74; on background #050505
Calculated contrast ratio: approximately 4.2:1 (below 4.5:1 requirement)
findings.impact: Users with low vision may have difficulty reading text marked with --text-dim color. This violates WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) (AA).

findings.title: Touch target size below 44x44 CSS pixels for some buttons
findings.description: Some interactive elements in the admin panel appear to have sizes below the recommended 44x44 CSS pixel minimum for touch targets. Buttons with classes like btn-sm may have insufficient size.
findings.evidence: .btn-sm class exists but specific small buttons for actions like delete, edit, etc. appear undersized based on typical admin interface patterns.
findings.impact: Users with motor disabilities or using touch devices may have difficulty accurately targeting small buttons. This is a best-practice issue for AAA compliance, though functional for AA.

findings.title: Modal dialogs may have focus trap issues
findings.description: The admin panel contains modal dialogs (e.g., create-modal with role="dialog"), but it's unclear if focus is properly trapped within the modal and if Escape key closes it. Focus management in modals is critical for keyboard accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Implement focus trap and Escape key handling:
1. On modal open: save focus, move focus to first focusable element in modal, trap focus within modal
2. On Escape key: close modal and restore focus to trigger element
3. Ensure all focusable elements within modal are keyboard accessible
findings.impact: If focus is not properly managed, keyboard users may become trapped or confused navigating modals. Escape key may not work as expected. This violates WCAG 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap (A).

findings.title: Logo/brand mark lacks alt text or aria-label
findings.description: In the marketing site and some pages, the logo mark (span with text 'AS' or 'A') appears without an alt text or aria-label, making it inaccessible to screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden if decorative: A Or add aria-label if functional: A
findings.evidence: A
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot identify the brand/logo. If the logo is purely decorative and conveyed by surrounding text, it should be aria-hidden. If it's functional, it needs an aria-label.

findings.title: Image alt text could be more descriptive
findings.description: Multiple images have alt text that is just the filename (e.g., alt="p.filename"). While not empty, this is not descriptive enough for users to understand the content.
findings.fix_suggestion:
For photo gallery thumbnails, use more descriptive alt text:
  alt="Thumbnail: ${esc(p.filename)}"
Or provide context through surrounding text and use a descriptive aria-label for important images.
findings.impact: Screen reader users get less meaningful descriptions of images. While technically compliant (alt text exists), this reduces usability. Violates WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (A) best practices.

findings.title: Possible positive tabindex usage
findings.description: While most elements use tabindex="0" correctly, some dynamically generated elements may have tabindex values that could interfere with natural focus order.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Search for and remove all positive tabindex values (tabindex="1", tabindex="2", etc.):
  - Use tabindex="0" for elements that should be in natural tab order
  - Use tabindex="-1" for elements that should be programmatically focusable but not in tab order
findings.evidence:
tabindex="0" on gallery-card (correct)
But need to verify no positive tabindex (> 0) is used elsewhere
findings.impact: Positive tabindex values can break the natural focus order, confusing keyboard users. WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order (A) requires logical focus order.

findings.title: Auto-updating content in admin panel needs pause/stop controls
findings.description: The admin panel likely auto-updates various elements (job status, viewer activity, etc.), but there's no visible pause/stop control for this auto-updating content. This can be disorienting for some users.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add a pause/stop button for auto-updating content:
  <button id="pause-auto-update" aria-label="Pause auto-updating content">Pause Updates</button>

Implement control to toggle auto-update behavior.
findings.evidence: Appears to have polling/auto-update mechanisms based on JavaScript but no pause control visible
findings.impact: Users with cognitive disabilities or those using screen readers may find auto-updating content disorienting or cause them to lose their place. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide (A).

findings.title: Form error messages not always identified in text
findings.description: Error messages appear in the UI but may rely on color alone (red text) without textual identification or aria-live regions to announce errors to screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Wrap error messages in aria-live regions:
When setting error text: document.getElementById('form-errors').textContent = 'Email is required.';
findings.evidence: Error messages appear to be displayed but need verification that they use aria-live="polite" or aria-live="assertive"
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not hear error messages immediately. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification (A).

findings.title: Fixed width layouts may break reflow at 320px width
findings.description: Some elements appear to use fixed widths or may not scale well at very narrow viewports (320px width or 400% zoom). This can cause horizontal scrolling and content loss.
findings.evidence: .login-inner { width: 100%; max-width: 380px; } - This max-width may be too restrictive on very narrow screens
findings.impact: Users on very narrow mobile devices or with 400% zoom may encounter horizontal scrolling and difficulty accessing content. This violates WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow (AA).

findings.title: Range sliders may lack keyboard labels
findings.description: Input range sliders (e.g., chc-slider, wiz-strength) are used for photo strength and other controls, but may lack associated labels or aria-labels explaining their purpose.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add associated label:
  <label for="wiz-strength">Strength (0-100%)</label>
  <input type="range" id="wiz-strength" min="0" max="100" value="75" aria-label="Strength: 75%">

Or add aria-label:
  <input type="range" aria-label="ChromaHaus strength adjustment (0-100%)" ...>
findings.evidence: <input type="range" class="chc-slider" id="wiz-strength" min="0" max="100" value="75">
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not understand the purpose of the slider control. This violates WCAG 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions (A).

findings.title: Language attribute on html element verified
findings.description: All pages have lang="en" attribute, which is correct. However, any content in non-English languages should have lang attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed. Ensure any non-English content has appropriate lang attributes.
findings.evidence: <html lang="en"> - Correct
findings.impact: No negative impact. This is compliant.

findings.title: Feedback form has good structure with labels
findings.description: The feedback.html form properly associates labels with inputs using for/id attributes and includes aria-live for status messages. Good accessibility pattern.
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue using this pattern for all forms.
findings.impact: Positive - good accessibility implementation.

findings.title: Text appears to be rendered as actual text, not images
findings.description: Good practice - text is rendered as HTML text, not as images. This supports user text resizing and is good for accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue using HTML text instead of image-based text.
findings.evidence: All text is HTML text, not image-based
findings.impact: Positive - supports text resizing and zoom. Compliant with WCAG 1.4.5 Images of Text (AA).

findings.title: Label text visibility for input controls
findings.description: Form inputs use placeholder text which is not ideal for accessibility. While some inputs have associated labels via aria-label, visible labels would be better.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add visible labels in addition to aria-label:
  <label for="key-input">Admin key</label>
  <input type="password" id="key-input" placeholder="Paste your key" aria-label="Admin key">
findings.evidence: <input type="password" id="key-input" placeholder="Admin key" aria-label="Admin key">
findings.impact: While aria-label is present (good), visible labels provide better accessibility and usability for all users, including those with cognitive disabilities.

findings.title: Autocomplete attributes on form inputs
findings.description: Many form inputs use autocomplete attributes (autocomplete="email", autocomplete="current-password"), which is good for accessibility and usability.
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue using appropriate autocomplete attributes for personal data fields.
findings.evidence: autocomplete="email", autocomplete="current-password" found in multiple inputs
findings.impact: Positive - autocomplete helps users with motor disabilities and improves usability. Compliant with WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose (AA).

findings.title: Page titles are descriptive
findings.description: All pages have descriptive <title> elements that accurately reflect page content. Good compliance with WCAG 2.4.2 Page Titled (A).
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue using descriptive page titles for all pages.
findings.evidence: <title>Analogify Studio</title>
findings.impact: Positive - descriptive titles help users understand page context. Compliant with WCAG 2.4.2 Page Titled (A).

findings.title: No auto-playing audio or video detected
findings.description: No background audio or auto-playing video detected in the frontend. Good compliance with WCAG 1.4.2 Audio Control (A).
findings.fix_suggestion: If auto-playing media is added in future, ensure pause/stop controls are available.
findings.evidence: No <audio> or <video> tags with autoplay attribute found
findings.impact: Positive - no auto-playing content that could be disorienting or confusing for users with cognitive disabilities.

findings.title: Focus does not trigger unexpected context changes
findings.description: No evidence of focus handlers that trigger page navigation or unexpected changes. Good compliance with WCAG 3.2.1 On Focus (A).
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue to avoid using onfocus handlers for navigation or form submission.
findings.evidence: No onfocus handlers detected that trigger navigation or form submission
findings.impact: Positive - focus handling is predictable and doesn't surprise users.

findings.title: HTML structure appears to have valid nesting
findings.description: HTML elements appear to be properly nested with correct structure. Good compliance with WCAG 4.1.1 Parsing (A).
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue using semantic HTML and proper element nesting.
findings.evidence: Proper use of <main>, <section>, <button>, form elements with correct nesting
findings.impact: Positive - valid HTML structure supports screen reader parsing and interpretation.

summary.notes: This is a backend API service with minimal user-facing HTML. The primary findings relate to HTML email templates. API responses return JSON only. Email accessibility constraints differ from web accessibility.

findings.title: Email templates missing lang attribute
findings.description: The HTML email templates do not declare a language using the lang attribute. While this is a common limitation in email clients, screen readers and language-detection tools may incorrectly interpret the content language, particularly affecting international users or when emails are archived in systems that perform language detection.
findings.fix_suggestion: Wrap email templates in a complete HTML document with lang attribute: `<html lang="en"><head><meta charset="UTF-8"></head><body>...content...</body></html>`. Note: Many email clients strip outer HTML tags, so this may need to be implemented at the email service level (Resend API offers html composition options).
findings.evidence: <div style="font-family:system-ui,sans-serif;max-width:480px;margin:0 auto;padding:2rem;">
findings.impact: Screen reader users with assistive technology set to a different language may hear incorrect pronunciation. Machine translation and language detection tools may produce incorrect results.

findings.title: Inconsistent heading hierarchy in email templates
findings.description: Email templates start with `<h2>` as the main heading instead of `<h1>`. This violates proper heading hierarchy (WCAG 1.3.1) and can confuse screen reader users about the document structure. While email clients have constraints, proper heading hierarchy should be maintained where possible.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Change `<h2>` to `<h1>` in all email templates to establish proper heading hierarchy. The specific lines:
- index.mjs line 379: Change `<h2>` to `<h1>`
- index.mjs line 517: Change `<h2>` to `<h1>`
- index.mjs line 692: Change `<h2>` to `<h1>`
- index.mjs line 798: Change `<h2>` to `<h1>`
- admin.mjs line 248: Change `<h2>` to `<h1>`
findings.evidence: Welcome to ${branding.name}
findings.impact: Screen reader users navigating by heading structure may be confused about content hierarchy. Users relying on heading navigation to quickly scan emails may miss the main content.

findings.title: Email templates lack semantic document structure
findings.description:
The HTML email templates consist only of inline `
` and `

` elements with inline styles, with no proper semantic HTML document structure (no ``, ``, ``, or `` tags). While many email clients strip these tags, proper structure improves compatibility with screen readers and email clients that preserve semantic meaning.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Wrap all email template HTML in a proper document structure:
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>Email Title</title>
</head>
<body>
  <main role="main">
    <!-- existing template content -->
  </main>
</body>
</html>
```
Note: Email clients may strip outer HTML, so verify with Resend API documentation for HTML structure support.
findings.evidence:
...

...

...
findings.impact: Email clients and accessibility tools that attempt to parse the HTML may fail to identify logical sections or structure. Screen readers may have difficulty determining the proper reading order and content hierarchy.

findings.title: Matrix cells are clickable but lack proper button semantics
findings.description: The score matrix in the dashboard contains clickable span elements inside table cells with click event handlers. These cells are not keyboard-accessible because span elements are not focusable and do not trigger click handlers on Enter/Space keys. Screen reader users cannot determine that these cells are interactive.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change clickable span elements to proper button elements with role="button" or convert to actual <button> tags. Add keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space) to trigger the same click behavior.
findings.evidence: 85
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users and screen reader users cannot interact with the matrix cells to view department scores for each repository. This blocks access to core functionality on the HQ dashboard.

findings.title: Severity and status information conveyed by color alone
findings.description: Throughout the dashboard, critical information is conveyed using color only without accompanying text. The matrix uses color-coded cells (green=high scores, red=low scores) with numeric values only. The severity badges use color but rely on context to understand meaning.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add text labels in addition to color. For severity badges: Critical. For matrix cells, add aria-label with text description.
findings.impact: Color-blind users cannot distinguish between different severity levels or understand matrix cells. This blocks access to critical information about system health and prioritization.

findings.title: Filter toggle element lacks keyboard and ARIA semantics
findings.description: The filter toggle labeled 'AI Fixable' (#filterAiFixable) is a div element with click handler but no keyboard support or ARIA role. It cannot be accessed via keyboard and screen readers cannot identify it as an interactive control.
findings.fix_suggestion: Convert to a proper checkbox input with label:
findings.evidence: AI Fixable
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users cannot toggle the 'AI Fixable' filter. Screen reader users cannot identify this as a control. This blocks filter access for multiple user types.

findings.title: Missing skip navigation link and landmark regions
findings.description: The dashboard lacks a skip-to-main-content link as the first focusable element, and the main content area is not wrapped in a semantic <main> landmark. No semantic landmark elements (<nav>, <main>, <header>, <footer>) exist to help screen reader users navigate page structure.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add a skip link as the first element in body: Skip to main content. Wrap main content in
. Use semantic landmarks.
findings.evidence:
<body>
<!-- Top Bar (fixed) -->
<div class="topbar">
findings.impact: Screen reader users must navigate through all top bar buttons and selects before reaching main content. No clear page structure is communicated to assistive technology users.

findings.title: Slide-over panels lack keyboard and focus management
findings.description: The slide-over panels are shown/hidden with display:none/block, but there is no visible focus management or Escape key handler. When panels open, focus management is unclear, creating potential confusion for keyboard users.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add JavaScript to manage focus: move focus to panel on open, handle Escape key to close, trap focus within panel, and restore focus when closed.
findings.evidence:
<div class="slideover" id="slideover">
  <div class="slideover-header">
    <button class="slideover-close" id="slideoverCloseBtn">×</button>
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users may become confused about focus location when panels open. Users may have difficulty understanding where they are or how to close the panel.

findings.title: Icon-only close button lacks accessible label
findings.description: Close buttons use only the '×' symbol with no accessible label. Screen readers will announce this as 'times' or remain silent, leaving users unclear about the button's purpose.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-label to all close buttons: <button class="slideover-close" aria-label="Close panel">×</button>
findings.evidence: <button class="slideover-close" id="slideoverCloseBtn">×</button>
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot understand that this button closes the panel. This affects usability of all modal panels on the dashboard.

findings.title: Table missing proper header scope attributes
findings.description: The matrix table uses <th> elements for headers but lacks scope attributes to indicate whether each header is a row or column header. This affects data table accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add scope attributes to table headers: <th scope="col">QA</th> for column headers and <th scope="row">repo-name</th> for row headers.
findings.evidence:
<table class="matrix" id="matrixTable">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Product</th>
      <th>QA</th>
      <th>SEO</th>
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot easily understand table structure. When navigating cells, assistive technology cannot announce which column/row the cell belongs to.

findings.title: Potential color contrast issues in dark theme
findings.description: Several color combinations may not meet WCAG AA contrast ratio of 4.5:1. The text color var(--text-dim) (#8888a0) on surface background (#12121a) is borderline. Some badges and labels use even dimmer colors.
findings.fix_suggestion: Conduct contrast testing on all text color combinations using WCAG AA standards (4.5:1). Adjust color values if needed or increase font-size of dim text.
findings.evidence:
--text-dim: #8888a0; /* on background #12121a */
--text-muted: #5a5a70; /* even dimmer */
findings.impact: Low-vision users may struggle to read text using dim color variants, particularly in FAQs and documentation sections.

findings.title: Text may not resize properly at 200% zoom
findings.description: The dashboard uses rem-based sizing which is technically resizable, but many very small sizes (0.65rem, 0.68rem) become illegible at high zoom. Some inline styles use fixed measurements.
findings.fix_suggestion: Test dashboard at 200% zoom to ensure no content loss or layout breakage. Consider increasing minimum font sizes for body text.
findings.evidence:
.score-card-name { font-size: 0.72rem; }
.timestamp { font-size: 0.72rem; }
findings.impact: Users who need to increase text size to 200% zoom may see overlapping text, truncation, or loss of content in some areas.

findings.title: Filter inputs lack visible labels
findings.description: The filter selects in the slide-over panel have aria-label attributes but no visible <label> elements. While aria-label provides screen reader text, there is no visible label for sighted users.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add visible labels for form controls. Wrap each select with a label element or use fieldset/legend structure.
findings.evidence:
<select class="filter-select" id="filterSeverity" aria-label="Filter by severity">
  <option value="all">All Severities</option>
findings.impact: New users may not understand the purpose of each filter without visible labels. While option text provides some context, explicit labels are clearer.

findings.title: Copy-to-clipboard interaction provides no screen reader feedback
findings.description: The command text in the finding detail panel has a title attribute 'Click to copy' but provides no feedback to screen readers when copy succeeds. Text content changes are not announced to assistive technology.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use aria-live region to announce copy confirmation:
. Update it when copy succeeds.
findings.evidence:
var cmdSpan = el('span', {className: 'ai-fix-cmd', title: 'Click to copy'});
...cmdSpan.textContent = 'Copied!';
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot hear confirmation that copy succeeded. Users may not realize the interaction happened.

findings.title: Section labels are not semantic headings
findings.description: Section labels like 'Product x Department' and 'Needs Attention' use <div class="section-label"> instead of proper heading elements. These lack semantic meaning and cannot be used for navigation.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use semantic heading elements:

Product x Department

. Ensure proper heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence: Product x Department
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot use heading navigation to jump between sections. Page structure is unclear to assistive technology.

findings.title: Dialog/overlay elements lack proper ARIA roles
findings.description: The slide-over and detail panels use <div> elements with display:none/block styling but lack role="dialog" or other ARIA roles to indicate their purpose to screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add ARIA roles:
and
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not recognize these as dialog or overlay components. The modal nature of panels is not communicated.

findings.title: Focus indicators lack sufficient visibility
findings.description: No explicit focus styles are defined in the CSS. Browser defaults will apply, which may not be sufficiently visible against the dark background.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add explicit focus styles: *:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }
findings.evidence: CSS contains no :focus-visible styles with sufficient contrast
findings.impact: Keyboard users may lose track of where they are on the page when tabbing, particularly in the dark interface.

findings.title: Auto-refresh dashboard may affect accessibility
findings.description: Dashboards auto-refresh every 60 seconds. Rapidly changing content can disrupt screen reader users mid-navigation or announce updates unexpectedly.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add pause refresh button or allow users to disable auto-refresh. Use aria-live="polite" for updates without interrupting navigation.
findings.evidence: Department dashboards auto-refresh data every 60 seconds
findings.impact: Screen reader users may experience interrupted reading or unexpected content changes. Users with attention disabilities may find frequent updates distracting.

findings.title: Documentation grid layout lacks semantic structure
findings.description: The docs-content.html uses CSS grid layout with <div> elements instead of semantic table or proper structure to organize information.
findings.fix_suggestion: If presenting structured data, use proper table elements with th/td and scope attributes. For layout grids, add heading elements.
findings.evidence:
<div class="docs-grid">
  <div>...
  <div>...
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot easily determine the relationship between content columns.

summary.overall_assessment: The CertStudy application demonstrates good accessibility foundation with proper semantic HTML, focus management in modals, and ARIA labels on interactive elements. However, there are several medium-severity issues around animation support for motion sensitivity, color-only indicators, and keyboard accessibility for nested interactive elements.
strengths: Skip-to-content link properly implemented with sr-only and focus styles
Modal focus trapping implemented in WhatsNewModal, KeyboardShortcuts, TutorialOverlay
Form labels correctly associated with inputs (htmlFor/id pairs)
Navigation menu keyboard accessible with proper aria-expanded state
aria-labels present on 22+ interactive icon buttons
Error messages use role='alert' where appropriate
Color contrast appears adequate in both light and dark themes
Tab trap prevention logic in place for keyboard navigation
weaknesses: Non-semantic divs with onClick lack keyboard support
Modal implementations lack inert attribute for background
Color-only indicators for difficulty and status
Form errors not linked to inputs via aria-describedby
Some icon buttons may be below 44x44px minimum tap target
Keyboard shortcuts help hidden behind ? hotkey

wcag_compliance.rationale: Meets WCAG 2.1 Level AA criteria overall. Critical accessibility features are in place (keyboard navigation, focus management, semantic HTML), but medium-severity issues prevent AAA compliance.

findings.title: Non-semantic click handlers on div elements lack keyboard support
findings.description: Two div elements with onClick handlers in FlashcardCard.tsx (lines 102, 114) do not have keyboard event handlers. While the parent card is keyboard accessible via role='button', these nested divs with onClick are only mouse-accessible.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add onKeyDown handler to these divs, or better: wrap the VoiceReader and SaveFactButton in properly semantic elements, or use event delegation on the parent button.
findings.evidence: div.mt-2.flex.justify-center with onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()} — no onKeyDown handler
findings.impact: Users relying on keyboard navigation cannot interact with voice reading buttons inside the flashcard front/back sections.

findings.title: Modal overlays may lack inert attribute for background content
findings.description: Three modal/dialog components (WhatsNewModal, KeyboardShortcuts, TutorialOverlay) implement focus trapping but do not use the inert attribute on the rest of the page. This means background elements are still technically focusable via Tab if the focus trap logic has any gaps.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add inert={true} to the main content container when modal is open, or use a library that handles this automatically. Alternatively, ensure all focusable elements outside the modal are unreachable.
findings.evidence: Dialog implementations use manual focus trap but no inert on body or sibling elements
findings.impact: Users with assistive technology or keyboard navigation might be able to interact with background content while a modal is open, causing confusion about focus state.

findings.title: Color alone conveys quiz difficulty and domain progress information
findings.description: In QuizQuestion.tsx, difficulty levels are shown with color-only indicators (easy=green, medium=amber, hard=red). Domain progress indicators in insights use color heat maps without text alternatives.
findings.fix_suggestion: Ensure difficulty labels (easy/medium/hard) are always displayed as text, not just color. For heatmaps, add tooltips or aria-labels describing the intensity level.
findings.evidence: className with bg-green-600/10, bg-amber-600/10, bg-red-600/10 but the difficulty level text is within the colored badge
findings.impact: Users with color blindness or low vision cannot distinguish difficulty levels or study progress without color perception.

findings.title: Animations and transitions do not pause with prefers-reduced-motion
findings.description: CSS includes @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) in globals.css (line 73-79), but Framer Motion animations in page.tsx, QuizPageClient.tsx, and other components do not check this preference. Users with vestibular disorders see full animations regardless of OS accessibility setting.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create a custom hook to check prefers-reduced-motion and conditionally disable Framer Motion animations. Or use Framer Motion's built-in support via custom variants.
findings.evidence: motion.div with animate, initial, exit props without checking window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')
findings.impact: Users with vestibular disorders, epilepsy, or motion sensitivity experience animations that can cause discomfort or dizziness.

findings.title: Reading gauge SVG lacks complete ARIA description
findings.description: ReadinessGauge.tsx uses role='img' with aria-label, but the SVG is decorative-plus-informative. The aria-label only contains the percentage but the actual readiness status text below is separate.
findings.fix_suggestion: Enhance aria-label to include the status: `Readiness: ${Math.round(readiness)}% — ${status}`. Or use aria-describedby to link the SVG to the status text.
findings.evidence: SVG with role='img' aria-label={`Readiness: ${Math.round(readiness)}%`} but status text like 'Exam ready' is in separate span
findings.impact: Screen readers announce the percentage but the status interpretation might be unclear. The visual gauge and text are logically grouped but semantically separated.

findings.title: Form error messages not explicitly linked to inputs via aria-describedby
findings.description: LoginPageClient, RegisterPage, and UnlockModal display form errors visually below inputs, but error text is not associated to the input field via aria-describedby attribute.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add aria-describedby on the input pointing to a unique id on the error message paragraph. Example: ...

{errors.email}
findings.evidence:
label + input followed by conditional error

, but no aria-describedby linking them
findings.impact: Screen reader users are not explicitly informed that the error message is associated with the specific input field.

findings.title: Icon-only buttons lack visual distinction at rest
findings.description: Several icon-only buttons (theme toggle, menu toggle in Navigation.tsx) rely on hover color change but lack a clear border or background to indicate they are interactive elements at rest.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a subtle border or outline at rest (e.g., border-1 border-card-border) or increase padding. Ensure icon contrast is at least 3:1.
findings.evidence: button with p-2 rounded-lg, no visible border or background at rest
findings.impact: Low-vision users may not immediately recognize these as buttons without clear visual affordance.

findings.title: Some interactive elements below 44x44px minimum tap target
findings.description: Multiple icon buttons and interactive elements use padding p-1 or p-2, which may result in tap targets below the 44x44 pixel minimum recommended for mobile accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase padding to p-2.5 or p-3 on all interactive elements. Use tap-target class consistently (already defined in globals.css).
findings.evidence: p-1 and p-2 padding on icon buttons (approximately 16-24px effective size)
findings.impact: Mobile and touch device users may have difficulty accurately tapping small buttons, especially those with motor control impairments.

findings.title: Keyboard shortcut modal hidden behind ? key with no discoverable trigger
findings.description: KeyboardShortcuts component is only accessible via ? key press. There is no menu item, help link, or accessible discovery mechanism for users who don't know this hotkey exists.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a '?' or 'Help' icon/button in the navigation or settings menu that displays the shortcuts dialog. Or ensure it's mentioned in the tutorial overlay.
findings.evidence: Component only listens for '?' key, no visual affordance or menu entry
findings.impact: Users unfamiliar with the interface cannot discover or access the keyboard shortcuts guide.

findings.title: Language attribute correct on html element, but no preparation for multilingual content
findings.description: Root layout.tsx correctly sets lang='en' on the html element. Currently the app is English-only, but no infrastructure for marking language changes if multilingual content is added.
findings.fix_suggestion: No immediate action needed. When adding non-English content, use lang attribute on specific elements. Document this as a future consideration.
findings.evidence: html lang='en' is set correctly
findings.impact: Currently minimal impact as the app is English-only.

findings.title: Main content uses fixed max-width, potential horizontal scroll on very small viewports
findings.description: layout.tsx applies inline style maxWidth='512px' which may cause horizontal overflow on very small devices (< 320px) without proper responsive handling.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use responsive max-width via CSS media query or Tailwind responsive classes. Ensure no horizontal scroll at 320px viewport.
findings.evidence: style={{ maxWidth: '512px', marginLeft: 'auto', marginRight: 'auto' }}
findings.impact: Users on very small phones or at 200%+ zoom may experience horizontal scrolling, violating WCAG 1.4.10.

findings.title: Toast notifications could better distinguish alert vs status with role attribute
findings.description: Toast.tsx uses aria-live='polite' on the container, which is correct. However, individual toasts do not have role='status' or role='alert' to distinguish notification types.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add role='status' for info/success, role='alert' for errors based on t.type property.
findings.evidence: div with aria-live='polite' but toasts within lack role='status' or role='alert'
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not clearly understand the priority/type of notification.

findings.title: No keyboard escape handling for navigation dropdown menu
findings.description: The dropdown menu in the navigation component does not respond to the Escape key to close the menu. Users relying on keyboard navigation must use Tab or click elsewhere to close the dropdown.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a script to handle Escape key on the dropdown menu to close it and return focus to the trigger button.
findings.evidence: .nav-dropdown-menu shows/hides via :hover/:focus-within/class but has no keydown listener for Escape key
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users cannot efficiently navigate dropdown menus. Screen reader users may struggle with menu closure.

findings.title: Mobile menu does not trap focus or restore focus on close
findings.description: When the mobile navigation menu is opened, focus is not trapped within the menu overlay, and when closed, focus is not returned to the hamburger menu button.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add focus management to trap focus within the menu and return focus to the toggle button when menu closes.
findings.evidence: .nav-toggle button shows .nav-links overlay but does not manage focus or set aria-modal
findings.impact: Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile menu when it's open. Screen reader users cannot understand that a modal is open.

findings.title: Scroll pulse animation lacks aria-hidden attribute
findings.description: The scroll indicator animation at the bottom of the hero section animates continuously but is not marked as decorative.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-hidden="true" and role="presentation" to mark the scroll indicator as decorative.
findings.evidence: <div class="scroll-indicator fade-in fade-in-delay-4"> animates with @keyframes scrollPulse but has no aria-hidden
findings.impact: The scrolling animation may be announced to screen reader users or misinterpreted as interactive, causing confusion.

findings.title: Scroll-triggered parallax effect lacks complete prefers-reduced-motion support
findings.description: While global prefers-reduced-motion is respected in JavaScript, the parallax [data-parallax] attributes may still leave CSS transforms uncleared.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add CSS override to ensure parallax transforms are disabled: [data-parallax] { transform: none !important; } in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)
findings.evidence: if (!window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches) prevents listener but doesn't guarantee CSS cleanup
findings.impact: Users with vestibular disorders who enable prefers-reduced-motion may still experience motion effects.

findings.title: Form error messages rely on inline color styling
findings.description: Form validation error messages in the contact form are displayed via inline color styling without guaranteed visibility for users with color blindness.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add visual indicators beyond color, such as background color, border, or icon to distinguish error states.
findings.evidence: status.style.color = 'var(--red, #c9746b)' sets color via inline style without background or border indicators
findings.impact: Users with color vision deficiency may not perceive error messages. Users with CSS disabled may not see indications.

findings.title: Dropdown arrow correctly marked as decorative
findings.description: The dropdown arrow indicator is correctly marked with aria-hidden="true". Listed for completeness.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. Implementation is correct.
findings.evidence: ▾ correctly hidden from screen readers
findings.impact: No accessibility impact. This is correctly implemented.

findings.title: Form status region should use role="alert" for errors
findings.description: The form status message uses aria-live="polite" and role="status", but should use role="alert" for error messages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use role="alert" instead of role="status" for the status element.
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not be immediately notified of form submission errors.

findings.title: Project card borders have low contrast
findings.description: Project cards use --border-warm (#2a2318) on --bg-card (#111111) creating a contrast ratio of 2.1:1, below the 3:1 AA requirement.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase border contrast by using a lighter color (e.g., #3d3a30) or apply accent color on hover.
findings.evidence: border: 1px solid var(--border-warm) where colors are #2a2318 on #111111
findings.impact: Users with low vision may have difficulty distinguishing project card boundaries.

findings.title: Modal dialogs lack required ARIA attributes
findings.description: UnlockModal, RecoveryCodesModal, and EncryptionRecoveryModal components are missing critical ARIA attributes (role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby, aria-describedby) required to properly expose the dialog role and semantics to assistive technologies.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Update modal container with proper dialog attributes:

Unlock Journal

...
findings.evidence:
<div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/60 px-4">
      <div className="w-full max-w-sm rounded-2xl border border-card-border bg-surface p-6 shadow-xl">
findings.impact: Screen reader users won't understand these are modal dialogs. Focus management becomes unclear. Users may try to interact with background content. Keyboard navigation is compromised.

findings.title: Modal components lack focus trap and Escape key handling
findings.description: Modal dialogs (UnlockModal, RecoveryCodesModal) don't implement focus trapping or allow closing with Escape key. This creates keyboard navigation hazards where users can tab out of the modal to background content.
findings.evidence: Modal uses fixed positioning but has no focus management, no useEffect for Escape key listener, and no focus trap implementation.
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users cannot navigate effectively. Tab key sends focus to background elements. Screen reader users lose context when focus leaves modal. Escape key doesn't close modal.

findings.title: UnlockModal password input lacks associated label
findings.description: The password input in UnlockModal uses only a placeholder attribute ('Your password') without a proper <label> element. Placeholder text is not a substitute for labels as it disappears when the field is focused, leaving users without context.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add a proper label element:
<label htmlFor="unlock-password" className="mb-2 block text-sm font-medium text-foreground">Password</label>
<input
  id="unlock-password"
  type="password"
  value={password}
  onChange={e => setPassword(e.target.value)}
  placeholder="Enter your password"
  required
  autoFocus
  className="..."
/>
findings.evidence:
<input
            type="password"
            value={password}
            onChange={e => setPassword(e.target.value)}
            placeholder="Your password"
            required
            autoFocus
findings.impact: Users of assistive technology (screen readers) have no persistent label for this input. Sighted users with cognitive disabilities may lose context when typing into the field.

findings.title: Profile images in directory lack descriptive alt text
findings.description: Images of people in the attendee/speaker/vendor directory components lack descriptive alt text that identifies who the person is and their role.
findings.evidence:
<Image src={imageUrl} alt="" className="..." />
OR
<Image src={imageUrl} className="..." />
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot identify who appears in directory photos. Essential content (person identification) is completely lost for assistive technology users.

findings.title: Toast notifications lack role='status' attribute
findings.description: While Toast components have aria-live='polite', they should also have role='status' to properly announce status messages. This ensures the announcement is treated as a status update, not generic live region.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Update Toast container with explicit status role:
<div className="fixed bottom-4 right-4 z-[100] flex flex-col gap-2" role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true">
findings.evidence: <div className="fixed bottom-4 right-4 z-[100] flex flex-col gap-2" aria-live="polite">
findings.impact: Toast notifications may not be announced consistently to all screen reader users. Role='status' makes the purpose explicit to assistive technology.

findings.title: RecoveryCodesModal missing dialog role and aria attributes
findings.description: RecoveryCodesModal is a modal dialog but lacks role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and aria-labelledby attributes needed for proper accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add ARIA dialog attributes:

Recovery Codes
findings.evidence:
<div className="fixed inset-0 z-50 flex items-center justify-center bg-black/60 px-4">
      <div className="w-full max-w-md rounded-2xl border border-card-border bg-surface p-6 shadow-xl">
findings.impact: Screen readers won't announce this as a dialog. Users may not understand the modal nature of the content.

findings.title: Login error message should use aria-live for dynamic announcement
findings.description: Error messages in the login form appear dynamically but lack aria-live='polite' to ensure screen readers announce the error when it appears.
findings.impact: Screen reader users must manually navigate to discover error messages. Errors aren't announced when they appear.

findings.title: Error text color contrast may be insufficient
findings.description: Error messages use red-400 (#f87171) text on a dark surface background (#10131a). The contrast ratio may not meet WCAG AA standards of 4.5:1 for normal text.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use a darker or more saturated red with higher contrast:
Option 1: Replace text-red-400 with text-red-500 or text-red-600
Option 2: Test contrast ratio and adjust to ensure >= 4.5:1
Recommended: <div className="...text-red-500"> or <div className="...text-red-600">
findings.evidence: <div className="...text-red-400">
findings.impact: Users with low vision or color blindness may not be able to perceive error messages clearly.

findings.title: Verify all form inputs have proper focus indicators
findings.description: Form inputs should have visible focus indicators. Check that CSS doesn't remove focus styling with outline:none without providing alternative focus indication.
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue using focus:border-accent or similar visible focus indicators. Current implementation is solid. Verify form inputs across the app maintain this pattern.
findings.evidence:
<input
              id="email"
              type="email"
              ...className="...outline-none transition-colors focus:border-accent"
findings.impact: While outline-none is used, focus:border-accent provides visible focus indication. This appears acceptable. Verify all inputs follow this pattern.

findings.title: Verify heading hierarchy follows logical structure
findings.description: Review heading levels across all pages to ensure they don't skip levels (e.g., h1 to h3 without h2) and maintain a logical document outline.
findings.fix_suggestion: Maintain consistent heading hierarchy across all pages: h1 for main title, h2 for major sections, h3 for subsections. Never skip levels.
findings.evidence:
Run your whole event from one mobile-first site.

...later...

{item.title}
findings.impact: Heading hierarchy in home-content appears correct (h1, then h2). Audit other pages for consistency.

findings.title: Text spacing adjustments should not cause content loss
findings.description: WCAG 2.1 AA requires that content remain visible and functional when users adjust text spacing (line-height 1.5x, letter-spacing 0.12em, word-spacing 0.16em, paragraph spacing 2x).
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue allowing text spacing adjustments. Verify overflow: hidden or fixed heights don't truncate text when spacing is increased.
findings.impact: No explicit text-spacing constraints found using !important that would prevent adjustment. Layout appears flexible.

findings.title: Missing aria-label on password visibility toggle button
findings.description: The Eye/EyeOff buttons in password fields have proper aria-labels ("Show password"/"Hide password"), which is correct. However, theme toggle button and other icon-only buttons throughout need verification. Icon-only buttons must have aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible text.
findings.fix_suggestion: The theme toggle button already has a proper aria-label. Verify all other icon-only buttons (close buttons, menu buttons, etc.) have similar labels.
findings.evidence:
<button onClick={toggleTheme} className="p-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-surface transition-colors tap-target" aria-label={`Switch to ${theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark'} mode`}>
  {theme === 'dark' ? <Sun size={18} /> : <Moon size={18} />}
</button>
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot understand the purpose of icon-only buttons without aria-labels, making navigation difficult.

findings.title: No skip-to-main-content link
findings.description: The application does not include a skip navigation link as the first focusable element. Users navigating by keyboard must tab through the entire navigation bar before reaching main content.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add skip link before Navigation: Skip to main content Add id="main-content" to
element.
findings.evidence: <body className="antialiased"><ThemeProvider><AuthProvider><ToastProvider><Navigation /><KeyboardShortcuts /><main className="pt-14 pb-24 min-h-screen max-w-lg mx-auto">{children}</main>
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users must navigate through all navigation links before reaching page content, slowing down navigation.

findings.title: Form validation errors conveyed by color only
findings.description: Form validation error messages in register/login pages are displayed in red (#ef4444) with no additional visual indicator beyond color. Users with color blindness cannot identify errors.
findings.evidence:
{errors.name &&

{errors.name}

}
findings.impact: Users with color vision deficiency (8% of males) cannot distinguish error states from normal fields.

findings.title: Icon buttons lack proper accessible names throughout app
findings.description: Multiple icon-only buttons throughout the application (dashboard cycle button, close buttons, etc.) lack proper aria-labels, making them inaccessible to screen reader users.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add aria-label to button:
aria-label={`Cycle macro display (currently showing ${config.label})`}
findings.evidence:
<button onClick={onTapLabel} className={`text-xs mt-0.5 ${config.color} tap-target`}>
  {config.label} {onTapLabel ? '▾' : ''}
</button>
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot understand button functions, requiring them to test each button to discover behavior.

findings.title: Information conveyed by color alone: macro nutrient colors
findings.description: The app uses color alone to distinguish macro nutrients (Protein=green, Carbs=amber, Fat=red, Water=blue). Users with color blindness cannot distinguish between nutrients.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add patterns, icons, or text labels in addition to color in charts and progress displays. Use SVG patterns or additional visual indicators.
findings.impact: Users with color vision deficiency (8% of males) cannot distinguish nutrients in charts, making the app's primary feature inaccessible.

findings.title: More drawer navigation lacks clear focus indicators
findings.description: Navigation drawer items don't show clear focus rings when navigating with keyboard. Users cannot see which item has focus.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add focus styles to Link:
focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent/50
findings.evidence:
<Link className={`flex flex-col items-center gap-1.5 p-3 rounded-xl transition-colors tap-target ${
  active ? 'bg-accent/10 text-accent' : 'text-muted hover:text-foreground hover:bg-surface'
}`}>
findings.impact: Keyboard users cannot identify focused navigation items, creating confusion about current position.

findings.title: Keyboard shortcut help reference unclear
findings.description: Keyboard shortcuts reference uses '?' as instruction text without clear context. Users may not understand it's a functional shortcut.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Use tag and clearer text:

Press ? to toggle shortcuts
findings.evidence: Press ? to toggle this overlay
findings.impact: Users unfamiliar with the app may not recognize '?' as a keyboard shortcut trigger.

findings.title: Toast notification buttons may be too small
findings.description: Undo button in toast notifications (text-xs) may not meet minimum 44x44px touch target size recommended for accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase button padding: className="px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-semibold rounded tap-target"
findings.evidence: <button className="text-xs font-semibold text-accent hover:text-accent-glow transition-colors tap-target">Undo</button>
findings.impact: Mobile users with motor control issues may have difficulty activating small buttons.

findings.title: Form inputs lack required field indicators
findings.description: Form fields (name, email, password) are marked as required in HTML but no visual indicator (asterisk, text) shows which fields are mandatory.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add visual required indicator:
findings.evidence:
<label htmlFor="name" className="block text-sm font-medium text-muted mb-1.5">
  Name
</label>
<input id="name" type="text" required ... />
findings.impact: Users may not immediately understand which form fields must be completed before submission.

findings.title: Error messages lack association with form fields
findings.description: Form error messages are displayed below inputs but not associated with the input using aria-describedby, limiting screen reader context.
findings.evidence:
{errors.name &&

{errors.name}

}
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot associate error messages with specific form fields.

findings.title: Decorative icons not explicitly marked as decorative
findings.description: Many decorative Lucide icons throughout app are not marked with aria-hidden="true", potentially being announced by screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add aria-hidden="true" to decorative icons:
<Flame ... aria-hidden="true" />
findings.evidence: <Flame className="text-accent mx-auto mb-4" size={56} />
findings.impact: Screen readers may announce icon names as separate elements, cluttering the experience.

findings.title: Feature grid section lacks proper heading structure
findings.description: Feature cards on landing page lack a section heading grouping them together. Only individual card h3 titles exist.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add section heading:

Key Features

{features}
findings.evidence:
{FEATURES.map((feature, i) => (

{feature.title}
findings.impact: Screen reader users don't understand that feature cards are grouped or the purpose of the section.

findings.title: Low-contrast focus indicators may be hard to see
findings.description: Focus indicators use green outline (#22c55e). While this provides ~4.8:1 contrast on dark backgrounds, on some lighter hover states it may be harder to see.
findings.impact: Users with low vision may have difficulty seeing focus indicators on certain backgrounds.

findings.title: Layout reflow at 400% zoom needs verification
findings.description: App uses max-w-lg (32rem) on main content. At 320px viewport (simulating 400% zoom), content must reflow without horizontal scroll.
findings.fix_suggestion: Test in DevTools at 320px viewport width, zoom 100%. If horizontal scroll appears, reduce max-width or padding on mobile.
findings.evidence: <main className="pt-14 pb-24 min-h-screen max-w-lg mx-auto">
findings.impact: Users with severe visual impairment who zoom to 400% may encounter horizontal scrolling.

findings.title: HTML language attribute correctly set
findings.description: The <html> element includes lang="en", correctly identifying page language as English.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence: <html lang="en">
findings.impact: Positive - screen readers will use English phonetic rules.

findings.title: Toast notifications auto-dismiss after delay
findings.description: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after 3-5 seconds. While this allows dismissal extension, the auto-dismiss timeout is good for accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed. Timeout is acceptable.
findings.evidence: const timer = setTimeout(() => onRemove(toast.id), toast.duration);
findings.impact: Positive - users can undo actions before timeout expires.

findings.title: Focus order is logical and sequential
findings.description: Review shows form inputs follow logical top-to-bottom tab order, and modals trap focus correctly.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence:
... name input
... email input
... password input
findings.impact: Positive - keyboard users can tab through forms logically.

findings.title: HTML structure uses unique element IDs
findings.description: All form inputs have unique id attributes (name, email, password, confirmPassword). No duplicate IDs detected.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence: id="name", id="email", id="password", id="confirmPassword"
findings.impact: Positive - HTML is well-formed.

findings.title: Form labels properly associated with inputs
findings.description: All form inputs use <label htmlFor="id"> correctly associated with input id attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence:
<label htmlFor="name">Name</label>
<input id="name" .../>
findings.impact: Positive - labels are accessible.

findings.title: Input autocomplete attributes prevent errors
findings.description: Login and register forms use proper autocomplete attributes (email, name, password) helping users and password managers.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence:
autoComplete="name"
autoComplete="email"
autoComplete="new-password"
findings.impact: Positive - helps users with motor impairments and password managers.

findings.title: No keyboard traps in modals or dialogs
findings.description: KeyboardShortcuts and WhatsNewModal properly trap focus within dialog and allow Escape to close.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.impact: Positive - users can escape from modals.

findings.title: Focus does not trigger unexpected context changes
findings.description: Form inputs and UI elements do not trigger navigation or submission on focus alone.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence: <input onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)} />
findings.impact: Positive - focus behavior is predictable.

findings.title: Input changes do not auto-submit forms
findings.description: Form inputs do not trigger auto-submission. Users must click submit button.
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence: <button type="submit" ...>Sign In</button>
findings.impact: Positive - users control submission timing.

findings.title: Form error messages are helpful and descriptive
findings.description: Register form provides descriptive error messages ('Password must be at least 8 characters', 'Passwords do not match').
findings.fix_suggestion: No change needed.
findings.evidence:
if (!password) newErrors.password = 'Password is required';
else if (password.length < 8) newErrors.password = 'Password must be at least 8 characters';
findings.impact: Positive - users understand what went wrong.

findings.title: Emoji used as graphics lack proper alternative text
findings.description: Login and register pages use emoji (📖 Bible) with role='img' and aria-label. While technically accessible, emojis should generally be replaced with proper icon components or SVGs with alt text. The emoji approach can cause rendering inconsistencies across platforms and screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Replace emoji with proper icon component from lucide-react: <BookOpen size={40} className="mx-auto text-gold" /> with proper aria-label if needed for decorative elements, or use aria-hidden if purely decorative.
findings.evidence: 📖
findings.impact: Screen reader users hear 'Bible' but may experience inconsistent rendering or pronunciation across devices. The emoji may display differently on various platforms, affecting perceived accessibility.

findings.title: Light mode accent color may have insufficient contrast
findings.description: In light mode, the accent color is #2563EB (blue) on backgrounds like #faf8f2 (warm parchment). The text at VersionSelector shows 'selected' buttons with border-accent and text-accent on light backgrounds. Contrast ratio may be below 4.5:1 for normal text depending on exact element styling.
findings.fix_suggestion: Test the light mode accent #2563EB against backgrounds and verify 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Consider darkening the accent to #1d4ed8 or lower for light mode. Update globals.css light theme section to use a darker blue accent.
findings.evidence: .light { --accent: #2563EB; } on background: #faf8f2
findings.impact: Users with low vision or color blindness may have difficulty distinguishing accent color elements from background, particularly on the light theme. This affects interactive elements and important UI indicators.

findings.title: Error messages rely primarily on color without text label
findings.description: Error states in forms (login, register, bookmarks deletion) use red background and red text. While the text contains the error message, the initial indication relies on color. Some error indicators in interactive states (e.g., delete confirmation buttons) use only red color without additional text indicators.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add an icon or text prefix before error messages. Example:
{error}
This provides non-color-dependent indication of error state.
findings.evidence: {error}
findings.impact: Users with color blindness may miss error notifications. The red color alone does not convey the error state to colorblind users without the text content, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (use of color).

findings.title: Loading spinner lacks accessible name
findings.description: Multiple pages use animated spinners (div with border and animation) to indicate loading state without accessible labels. Examples in login, register, daily verse page, and books page. These spinners have no aria-label, role, or visible text indicating loading status.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add accessibility attributes and optional visible text:
or with visible text:
Loading...
findings.evidence: <div className="h-8 w-8 animate-spin rounded-full border-2 border-accent border-t-transparent" />
findings.impact: Screen reader users are not informed that the page is in a loading state. They may attempt to interact with content that is not yet available, causing confusion.

findings.title: Menu button toggle state not visually indicated for keyboard users
findings.description: The mobile menu toggle button in Navigation uses aria-expanded, which is good, but the visual indicator is only the icon change (Menu to X). For keyboard-only users relying on focus indicators and not mouse hover, the expanded state may not be immediately clear when focused off the button.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add visual state indicator on the button itself when expanded. Update className to include conditional styles: className={`tap-target ... ${menuOpen ? 'bg-surface-hover' : ''}`} to provide visual feedback that the menu is open.
findings.evidence: aria-expanded={menuOpen} with icon change only, no background or border change when expanded
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users may not immediately perceive that the menu is open when they move focus away from the toggle button. Screen readers announce the state, but visual keyboard focus indicator alone doesn't show the menu state.

findings.title: Form inputs lack persistent visible labels in some cases
findings.description: Input fields in login, register, and reset-password pages have placeholder text but the placeholder disappears when the input is focused. The label is properly associated via htmlFor, but relies on placeholder text as a fallback. This can confuse users who focus on an input and forget what field they're in.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-describedby to inputs pointing to permanent label text, or float the label above the input when focused using CSS. Example: Keep the label visible outside the input field (current implementation is correct), ensure the label remains visible via CSS even when input is focused.
findings.evidence: <label htmlFor="email">Email</label><input placeholder="you@example.com" />. Placeholder disappears on focus.
findings.impact: Users may forget which field they are filling, especially with multiple form fields. While the label is technically present and associated, it's not visible while the field is focused, violating best practice for label visibility.

findings.title: Book dropdown lacks complete keyboard navigation support
findings.description: The books page has a dropdown for selecting books with filter tabs above it. The dropdown can be opened via click but arrow key navigation within the list is not evident from code inspection. The bookDropdownOpen state is managed but keyboard handlers for arrow keys to navigate items are not visible.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add keyboard event handlers for arrow keys in the dropdown. When dropdown is open, capture ArrowDown/ArrowUp to navigate items and Enter to select. Use useEffect with event listener for onKeyDown when bookDropdownOpen is true.
findings.evidence: Book dropdown UI uses button click to navigate but explicit arrow key support for cycling through books is not found in code.
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users may need to use Tab to cycle through many dropdown items instead of arrow keys, requiring more keystrokes to navigate the books list.

findings.title: Custom checkbox (mood selector) lacks proper ARIA attributes
findings.description: Journal page uses custom button elements styled as mood selectors with emoji. These are semantically buttons but do not expose the pressed/checked state via aria-pressed or similar ARIA attributes. Users can't determine which mood is currently selected via assistive technology.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-pressed attribute: <button aria-pressed={mood === m.value} onClick={() => setMood(m.value)} className="..."> to expose the selected state to screen readers.
findings.evidence: Mood buttons: <button onClick={() => setMood(m.value)} className={`... ${mood === m.value ? 'ring-2 ring-accent' : ''}`}>
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot determine which mood is currently selected. The visual ring indicator is not accessible to assistive technology users.

findings.title: Generic link text 'Read more' lacks context
findings.description: Blog preview links in home page use 'Read more' link text without aria-label or descriptive text to indicate what blog post they lead to.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-label to read more links: <Link href={item.href} aria-label={`Read more: ${item.title}`}> to provide context about the destination.
findings.evidence: Read more
findings.impact: Screen reader users encounter 'Read more' links without knowing what blog post they lead to. This reduces context and makes navigation harder for assistive technology users.

findings.title: Modal title not semantically marked as heading
findings.description: Tutorial, WhatsNew, and other modal components have titles styled as headings but use div elements instead of semantic h2/h3 tags.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use semantic heading tags: Change <div className="text-2xl font-bold"> to <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold"> to properly convey heading structure in modals.
findings.evidence:
{current.title}
- styled as heading but not a semantic tag
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not perceive the structure of modal content if headings are not semantically marked. Content appears as text rather than structural headings, affecting navigation.

findings.title: Filter tab changes not announced to screen readers
findings.description: Books page filter tabs (All Books, Old Testament, New Testament) trigger immediate filtering when clicked without announcing the change to screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add aria-live announcement after filter change to inform screen readers:
Showing {filterLabel} books
findings.evidence: onClick={() => { setFilter(f.key); setExpandedBook(null); setChapterVerses(null); }} - immediately changes visible content without announcement
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not be informed of the content change when filters are applied.

findings.title: Touch target size for close buttons may be below 44px
findings.description: Some icon-only close buttons (X icons) in modals use padding that may not meet the 44x44 minimum touch target size requirement for mobile accessibility.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use tap-target class or set min-h-11 min-w-11 on all interactive elements: <button className="tap-target rounded-lg..."
findings.evidence: <button className="rounded-lg bg-white/10 p-1.5"> - p-1.5 may result in <44px size
findings.impact: Mobile users with fine motor control difficulties may have trouble tapping small close buttons.

findings.title: Content reflow at 400% zoom may need verification
findings.description: Fixed-width max-w containers may cause horizontal scrolling at 400% zoom on narrow viewports.
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify responsive behavior at 400% zoom. Current design should handle this through responsive breakpoints.
findings.evidence: max-w-6xl container at high zoom may exceed viewport width on mobile devices
findings.impact: Users with low vision who zoom to 400% may encounter horizontal scrolling.

findings.title: Toast undo button lacks descriptive label
findings.description: Undo button in Toast component doesn't specify what is being undone.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-label: <button aria-label={`Undo: ${t.message}`} ...>Undo</button>
findings.evidence: <button>Undo</button> - lacks aria-label or aria-describedby
findings.impact: Screen reader users may activate Undo without knowing what it undoes.

findings.title: Dark mode card borders may lack sufficient contrast
findings.description: Card borders use --card-border (#1e2a4a) on background (#0a0f1e). The contrast may be insufficient for users with low vision.
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify contrast ratio and lighten border color if needed. Test with WebAIM contrast checker.
findings.evidence: --card-border: #1e2a4a; on background: #0a0f1e
findings.impact: Users may have difficulty distinguishing card borders and focus indicators.

findings.title: Multi-language content should have lang attributes
findings.description: App supports multiple Bible languages but doesn't mark different language content with lang attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add lang attributes to scripture in other languages if multi-language support is added: Hebrew text
findings.evidence: <html lang="en">
findings.impact: Screen readers will pronounce non-English content as English if lang attributes aren't set.

findings.title: Skip link implementation should be tested
findings.description: Skip link is implemented with sr-only class. Should verify it's the first focusable element and works on all browsers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify skip link is first focusable element and test with keyboard navigation.
findings.evidence: Skip to main content
findings.impact: If skip link isn't first, keyboard users must tab through navigation to reach main content.

findings.title: Light mode accent color contrast should be verified
findings.description: Light mode accent color (#2563EB) may not have sufficient contrast against light backgrounds (#faf8f2).
findings.fix_suggestion: Test contrast ratio of #2563EB on #faf8f2. If below 4.5:1, darken accent color for light mode.
findings.evidence: .light { --accent: #2563EB; } on background: #faf8f2
findings.impact: Low vision users may have difficulty reading accent-colored text in light mode.

findings.title: Form inputs have focus outline removed without replacement styling
findings.description: Multiple form inputs use focus:outline-none which removes default focus indicators. While global CSS defines :focus-visible styling, relying solely on border-color changes for focus indication may be insufficient.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove focus:outline-none from form inputs and rely on the global :focus-visible style. Alternative: add focus:outline focus:outline-2 focus:outline-accent
findings.evidence: className="... focus:outline-none"
findings.impact: Keyboard-only users may not see which form field is currently focused, making form completion difficult.

findings.title: Muted text color may have insufficient color contrast
findings.description: The --muted color (#9590A8) against dark background (#0c0a1a) may not meet 4.5:1 WCAG AA contrast requirement.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase lightness of muted color. Suggested: --muted: #b8aed8; or verify with contrast checker tool
findings.impact: Users with low vision may struggle to read secondary text throughout the application.

findings.title: CardBack component lacks proper aria-label in some instances
findings.description: CardBack rendered with onClick handlers may not have descriptive aria-labels.
findings.fix_suggestion: Provide aria-label when CardBack is used with onClick: <CardBack aria-label="Draw a new card" onClick={onClick} />
findings.evidence: export function CardBack({ onClick, size = 'md', glowing }: CardBackProps)
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot determine the purpose of interactive card elements.

findings.title: Very small text may be difficult to read
findings.description: Text sizes of 9px and 10px (including 'tap to reveal' instruction) may be hard to read at normal zoom.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase minimum text size to text-sm (14px) for instructional content
findings.evidence: className="text-[10px]" or text-xs for instructional text
findings.impact: Users with low vision may struggle to read important instructions.

findings.title: Toggle buttons lack aria-pressed state attribute
findings.description: Theme toggle and other toggle controls don't communicate their pressed/checked state to screen readers.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-pressed={theme === 'dark'} to theme toggle button
findings.evidence: <button onClick={toggle} ... aria-label={...}>
findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot determine if a toggle is on or off.

findings.title: Proper use of aria-hidden for decorative elements
findings.description: Decorative emoji are correctly marked with aria-hidden="true".
findings.fix_suggestion: Continue current pattern - no changes needed.
findings.evidence: aria-hidden="true" on decorative emoji
findings.impact: Decorative elements are properly hidden from screen readers.

findings.title: Required form fields lack visible required indicator
findings.description: Form inputs have required attribute but no visible indicator (asterisk or 'required' text).
findings.fix_suggestion: Add visible required indicator: * next to label or add '(required)' text
findings.evidence: required attribute without visible indicator
findings.impact: Users may not realize a field is required, leading to form errors.

findings.title: Some interactive elements may be below 44x44 touch target
findings.description: Small buttons may not meet 44x44px minimum touch target size.
findings.fix_suggestion: Increase padding to p-2.5 or p-3, or explicitly set min-height/min-width: 44px
findings.evidence: button className="tap-target rounded-lg p-2 ..."
findings.impact: Mobile users with motor impairments may have difficulty clicking small buttons.

findings.title: Card position labels may not be semantically associated
findings.description: Position labels like 'Past', 'Present', 'Future' are visually but not semantically linked to cards.
findings.fix_suggestion: Include position in card aria-label: aria-label={`${labels[i]}: ${getCardAriaLabel(card)}`}
findings.evidence: Position label displayed separately from card component
findings.impact: Screen reader users may not understand relationship between labels and cards.

findings.title: Expandable components may need aria-expanded
findings.description: Card detail expansions don't show aria-expanded state.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to expand button
findings.evidence: button onClick={() => setIsExpanded(!isExpanded)}
findings.impact: Screen readers may not convey expansion state.

findings.title: Skip to main content link is properly implemented
findings.description: Application includes invisible skip-to-main-content link that becomes visible on focus.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed - correctly implemented.
findings.evidence: sr-only focus:not-sr-only skip link
findings.impact: Keyboard users can skip navigation to reach main content.

findings.title: High contrast mode (AAA) not explicitly supported
findings.description: Current color scheme meets AA but not AAA enhanced contrast requirements.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider adding @media (prefers-contrast: more) support
findings.evidence: Color variables meet AA (4.5:1) but not AAA (7:1) standards
findings.impact: Aspirational improvement for users with severe low vision.

findings.title: Keyboard support for cards is properly implemented
findings.description: TarotCard and CardBack components support Enter and Space keys.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed - correctly implemented.
findings.evidence: onKeyDown handler with Enter/Space support
findings.impact: Keyboard users can fully interact with cards.

findings.title: Multiple form fields need visible focus indicators
findings.description: Form fields across reset-password, journal, and other pages use focus:outline-none.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove focus:outline-none or add focus:outline focus:outline-2 focus:outline-accent
findings.evidence: focus:outline-none in form inputs
findings.impact: Focus indication may be insufficient for some users.

findings.title: Reduced motion preferences are properly implemented
findings.description: Application correctly disables animations for users with prefers-reduced-motion.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed - correctly implemented.
findings.evidence: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) applied
findings.impact: Users with motion sensitivity are protected.

findings.title: Navigation has proper aria-current for active pages
findings.description: Navigation links correctly use aria-current="page".
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed - correctly implemented.
findings.evidence: aria-current={isActive ? 'page' : undefined}
findings.impact: Screen readers identify current page.

findings.title: Status messages use appropriate ARIA roles
findings.description: Toast notifications correctly use aria-live and role attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed - correctly implemented.
findings.evidence: role={toast.type === 'error' ? 'alert' : 'status'} aria-live
findings.impact: Screen reader users are notified of status changes.
